description_long: This object consists of two traditional, rectangular mailboxes mounted side by side on a sturdy vertical wooden post. The mailboxes are made of metal, each with a curved top and a front-facing door, and are painted in a dark gray color with some visible weathering. The post is supported by a square wooden enclosure filled with gravel or small stones, and four short wooden posts are positioned at each corner of the base for added stability. The mailboxes have small handles and what appear to be mail slots on the front, with faint identification numbers visible on each box. The entire structure is set on a concrete or asphalt pad, suggesting it is designed for outdoor, curbside use in an urban or suburban environment. No mail flags are visible on either mailbox.
description: A pair of dark gray metal mailboxes mounted on a wooden post, set within a square wooden and gravel base. The mailboxes have front doors with handles and faint identification numbers.
description_view_0: Front view showing both mailboxes, their curved tops, front doors, and the square wooden base with gravel and four corner posts.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the length of the mailboxes, the supporting post, and the rectangular base structure.
description_view_2: Opposite front view displaying the front doors of both mailboxes, visible handles, and identification numbers.
description_view_3: Rear side view showing the backs of the mailboxes and the supporting post, with the base and corner posts visible.
